message:
feat(abi): expand POSIX surface by 260+ symbols with conformance infrastructure

Major ABI expansion bringing exported symbol count from 1279 to 1539,
adding comprehensive POSIX compliance functions across all ABI modules:

ABI additions:
- unistd_abi: glibc stat wrappers (__xstat/__fxstat/__lxstat and 64-bit
  variants), DNS reentrant functions (gethostbyname2_r), ethernet database
  stubs, eaccess/euidaccess access checking, versionsort64
- pthread_abi: thread TID resolution, clock conversion to realtime, futex
  timed wait, RWlock timing variants, thread scheduling attribute functions
  (getinheritsched/setinheritsched), thread scope APIs
- signal_abi: XSI obsolete signal functions (siginterrupt, sighold, sigrelse,
  sigignore) for legacy compatibility
- startup_abi: program name globals (program_invocation_name, __progname with
  AtomicPtr), __cxa_thread_atexit_impl with TLS cleanup infrastructure
- stdio_abi, dirent_abi, wchar_abi, ctype_abi, locale_abi, process_abi,
  stdlib_abi, string_abi: targeted expansions for locale-aware variants,
  unlocked stdio, and C11 wide character functions

Infrastructure:
- version_scripts/libc.map: 357 new symbol exports including C++ ABI,
  GNU extensions, locale-aware wchar, ctype internal table accessors,
  and unlocked stdio functions
- healing_oracle: expanded canonical test suite from 7 to 20+ test cases
  with per-symbol/per-condition variants
- report.rs: new PosixConformanceReport infrastructure for machine-readable
  coverage reporting (bd-18qq.7 campaign) with case classification and
  quality flag tracking
- runtime_policy: deterministic evidence sampling with drive_decisions helper
- conformance fixtures: updated statistics (1168→1258 implemented symbols)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 5. **Hand off** - Provide context for next session
 
 
+---
+
+## cass — Cross-Agent Session Search
+
+`cass` indexes prior agent conversations (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, ChatGPT, etc.) so we can reuse solved problems.
+
+**Rules:** Never run bare `cass` (TUI). Always use `--robot` or `--json`.
+
+### Examples
+
+```bash
+cass health
+cass search "async runtime" --robot --limit 5
+cass view /path/to/session.jsonl -n 42 --json
+cass expand /path/to/session.jsonl -n 42 -C 3 --json
+cass capabilities --json
+cass robot-docs guide
+```
+
+### Tips
+
+- Use `--fields minimal` for lean output
+- Filter by agent with `--agent`
+- Use `--days N` to limit to recent history
+
+stdout is data-only, stderr is diagnostics; exit code 0 means success.
+
+Treat cass as a way to avoid re-solving problems other agents already handled.
+
 ---
